intro.speaker: melody
intro.dialogue: Before a single note plays, every song you've ever loved already has one thing going: a pulse. Feel that? That's what we're starting with.

melody.dialogue: A beat is a heartbeat, ja? Steady, warm, alive — it's the thing your foot taps to before you even notice you're doing it. Close your eyes and it just... pulls you along.

harmony.dialogue: To be precise: a beat is an evenly spaced pulse in time. Same gap, every hit, no exceptions. My sister feels it as a heartbeat — I measure it as a grid. Both descriptions are correct. We'll use the grid.

examples_bts.speaker: melody
examples_bts.dialogue: Here's the simplest possible pulse: one kick, spaced perfectly even. Hit play and feel it lock in.

bad_example.speaker: harmony
bad_example.dialogue: Now the wrong version — same four kicks, uneven spacing. Listen closely.
bad_example.error_explanation: The gaps aren't equal — two kicks land close together, then there's a long stretch of nothing. Your ear notices the inconsistency even if you can't say why. That's a broken pulse, not a beat.

test.dialogue: Your turn. Drag the kick onto the grid so it lands in a steady, even pulse — four hits, same gap every time.
hints: Try spacing the kicks evenly - the same gap between every hit.
Four kicks total, one on every other box: positions 1, 3, 5, and 7.

challenge.speaker: harmony
challenge.dialogue: If you want to push further: try a pulse that isn't every-other-box. Still even, just a different gap. See what still feels steady and what starts to feel broken.
challenge.goal: Build any evenly-spaced kick pattern that isn't the positions 0-2-4-6 you just used.

complete.speaker: melody
complete.dialogue: That's the pulse. Everything we build from here sits on top of it.
